queue.title: Canva subscription — API access + maximising value
queue.notes: Adam's Canva subscription just renewed. Before deciding whether to keep or cancel, research: 1) Does Canva have an API we can integrate with? (automated design generation, template filling, etc.) 2) General suggestions for how we can maximise value — social media graphics for AAD/SoulForge, portfolio materials, marketing assets, branded templates for collab pages, etc. Goal: justify the subscription or make an informed cancel decision.

queue.title: design and funcional inspo for our project management in the dash
queue.notes: An example setup that we can review and potentially adopt concepts from - and build into our own tools.

queue.title: Colour Theory & Visual Design — Foundation Research
queue.description: Deep dive into colour theory fundamentals, colour psychology, and practical application across web design and photography. Cover: colour wheel relationships (complementary, analogous, triadic), warm/cool theory, how status colour hierarchies work (green to amber to red), colour grading techniques in photography, and notable art movements that shaped modern design palettes. Practical focus — how to apply this, not just academic theory.
queue.notes: Adam wants to build understanding across photography and web design. Connect to design library entries. Focus on practical application.

queue.title: Intelligence Disciplines (INT Framework) — Deep Dive
queue.description: Research the full spectrum of intelligence disciplines: HUMINT, SIGINT, OSINT, IMINT, TECHINT, MASINT, FININT, GEOINT, COMINT, ELINT. Cover definitions, history, real-world examples, and the OSINT revolution (civilian access to intelligence tradecraft). Include the pizza theory history. Explore how intelligence briefing formats work (daily intel briefs, situation reports) as potential dashboard design inspiration.
queue.notes: Adam sees this as both a knowledge interest and practical application for dashboard command centre view. Assignment relationship between agents and INT types, not identity renaming.

queue.title: Offline Knowledge Archive — Foundation Research
queue.description: Research what a comprehensive offline knowledge archive should contain. Focus areas: (1) What prepper/resilience communities recommend as must-have offline references, (2) Best sources for downloadable survival/skills guides (public domain, Creative Commons), (3) Offline-friendly formats and organisation patterns, (4) Intelligence tradecraft manuals available publicly (FM 2-22.3, ATP 2-22.9, open OSINT guides, Bellingcat methodology), (5) Ham radio getting-started guides and Australian frequency allocations, (6) How to structure a markdown-based knowledge base that is navigable without search (good README index, cross-linking). Output a recommended folder structure and a prioritised list of content to acquire first.
queue.notes: Adam has a prepper mindset about offline information access. Start broad — survival, skills, tradecraft, comms. NAS is the target storage. Markdown is the format. This is an ongoing project, not a one-shot.

queue.title: Offline Knowledge Archive — Brand Name Research
queue.description: Come up with strong brand name options for the offline knowledge archive product. Requirements: (1) Intelligence/tradecraft flavour preferred but not mandatory, (2) Should evoke self-reliance, preparedness, offline capability, (3) Check each candidate against existing products, domains, trademarks — no clashes, (4) Check .com domain availability, (5) Consider how it sounds as a product name ("Get the [X]", "Download [X]"), (6) Shortlist 5-10 options with pros/cons and availability status. Think along lines like: Dead Drop, The Black Book, Field Manual, Signal Library, Dark Archive, Ground Truth, etc. Be creative but practical.
queue.notes: This will become a real product. Name matters. Intelligence community flavour is preferred. Check domains and existing products thoroughly.

queue.title: Prediction Markets in Australia (Polymarket Alternative)
queue.description: Research prediction markets accessible from Australia. Polymarket uses crypto/outcome betting on events (politics, tech, sports). Very aligned with our agent setup - Scout/Quinn could monitor trends and signal position changes near-instantly. Questions: Is Polymarket available in AU? Are there AU-specific alternatives? Legal status of prediction markets in Australia? Can AI agents place bets programmatically via API? What markets exist (politics, crypto, tech, sports)?
queue.notes: Adam sees strong connection between OpenClaw agent monitoring capabilities and prediction market positioning. Perfect Scout use case once online.

completed.title: Kimi K2.5 via NVIDIA NIM — setup and test
completed.notes: Source: https://build.nvidia.com/moonshotai/kimi-k2-thinking — We have NVIDIA API key. Kimi K2.5 available via OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Test tool calling reliability and evaluate as fallback option.
completed.findings: Kimi K2.5 (1T params, 32.86B activated, 200K context, multimodal) available FREE via NVIDIA NIM but with 30s+ latency (unusable for interactive use). OpenRouter offers same model at $0.60/$3.00/M with much better latency. Tool calling supported but has hallucination risk (may call undeclared tools without constrained decoding). NVIDIA API key confirmed in ~/.openclaw/.env. Recommendation: Add to fallback chain via OpenRouter (not NVIDIA free tier) after Sonnet, before Groq. Position: Opus → Sonnet → OpenRouter Kimi K2.5 → Groq Llama 3.3 70B. Best for: cost-sensitive workloads, long context, multimodal, coding. NOT suitable for: primary model, time-sensitive tasks, high-frequency tool calling, reasoning mode. Full setup guide, performance analysis, and implementation plan in memory/2026/03/2026-03-07.md

completed.title: Multi-Agent Command Dash Flows (X post by JulianGoldieSEO)
completed.notes: Source: https://x.com/JulianGoldieSEO/status/2028520820084244658 — Review for useful multi-agent patterns, command dash ideas we can apply to our setup.
completed.findings: Mission Control dashboard architecture for multi-agent visibility: Kanban board (backlog → in progress → review → done), live activity feed, agent profiles, approval flows. Key patterns: structured role definitions (not random prompts), org structure files for handoff sequences, tag-based coordination (agents tag next specialist when complete), heartbeat-driven work (agents check tasks automatically). Real workflows: content teams (project manager → researcher → writer → optimizer → distributor), system maintenance, multi-agent coding (planner → coder → tester → reviewer → security → PR agent). Critical insight from Silent Drift research: version system prompts like code, changelog for shared resources, health checks for tool references. Applied to Quinn+Forge: formalize with dashboard visibility, AUTONOMOUS.md + task-log.md split file pattern (Quinn reads goals, generates tasks, Forge appends only), morning task generation from goals, approval flows for Forge work. Full analysis in memory/2026/03/2026-03-06.md

completed.title: Free open-source TTS alternatives to ElevenLabs (Chatterbox, PocketTTS)
completed.notes: Reddit tip: 'no need to pay for ElevenLabs anymore — free open source alternatives like chatterbox or pocket tts.' Evaluate quality, ease of setup, OpenClaw compatibility.
completed.findings: Chatterbox by Resemble AI wins: beats ElevenLabs in blind tests (63.75% preference), MIT license, voice cloning from 5-10s audio (zero-shot), unique emotion control, ~200ms latency, built-in watermarking, 23+ languages, production-ready. Other contenders: Kokoro (82M params, 2-3GB VRAM, 210× realtime on 4090, runs on CPU, Apache 2.0, NO voice cloning but smallest/fastest), Qwen3-TTS (0.6B/1.7B, voice cloning from 3s, 10 languages, 97ms streaming, Apache 2.0). Cost comparison: Chatterbox FREE vs ElevenLabs $30-120 per 1M chars. Hardware: gaming laptop (RTX 3060+) runs all models, Mac M1-M4 works for Chatterbox+Kokoro. Recommendation: test Chatterbox-Turbo (350M, 4-8GB VRAM, lighter than full version), use for voice cloning Adam's voice for personalized TTS, keep Kokoro as fallback for low-resource situations. Impact: $0 cost, on-premise privacy, better quality, full emotion control, no rate limits. Full comparison table and setup path in memory/2026/03/2026-03-06.md

completed.title: Multi-Agent Pitfalls — Reddit wisdom (r/AI_Agents)
completed.notes: Source: https://www.reddit.com/r/AI_Agents/comments/1rhzp3g/the_part_of_multiagent_setups_nobody_warns_you/ — 'The part nobody warns you about.' Read and extract lessons applicable to our Quinn+Forge setup. Especially timing, comms, and error patterns.
completed.findings: Core problem: silent drift. Multi-agent setups degrade slowly (outdated context, file collisions, stale tool references). Solutions: version system prompts like code, changelog for shared resources, health checks for tool references, treat coordination as ops problem not prompt problem. Key insight: none break loudly, outputs just get worse over time. Applied to Quinn+Forge: need shared file protocol, weekly health checks, changelog for dashboard data writes. Full analysis in memory/2026/03/2026-03-05.md

completed.title: Alex Finn OpenClaw Use Cases — agent self-tasking from goals
completed.notes: Source: https://ucstrategies.com/news/6-openclaw-use-cases-that-actually-make-your-life-better-according-to-youtuber-alex-finn/ — Use Case #5 especially: agent reads goals and self-generates tasks. Research how to implement this for Forge. Also his Discord channel-per-agent architecture and 'Last 30 Days' research skill.
completed.findings: 6 workflows: Second Brain (text-based memory), Morning Brief (scheduled reports), Content Factory (multi-agent pipeline), Last 30 Days (Reddit/X research), Goal-Driven Self-Tasking (brain dump goals → agent generates 4-5 daily tasks autonomously), Mission Control (replace generic apps with agent-native tools). CRITICAL pattern: split task files to avoid race conditions - AUTONOMOUS.md (goals + open backlog, main session only) + tasks-log.md (append-only, sub-agents add lines only). Implementation plan: create AUTONOMOUS.md, add morning task gen to heartbeat, spawn Forge with append-only rule, build Kanban board in dashboard. Full analysis + implementation roadmap in memory/2026/03/2026-03-05.md

completed.title: Dashboard theme system architecture - multiple color themes and layout variations
completed.findings: Researched multi-theme architecture using CSS custom properties with data-theme attribute switching. 4 recommended themes: Mission Control (current), Midnight (dark purple/cyan), Solar (warm golden), Matrix (neon green). 3 layout variants: Cards (current grid), Compact (dense), Masonry (Pinterest-style). Implementation: CSS variables for colors, localStorage persistence, theme/layout switcher components. Chart.js colors need theme-aware injection. Analyzed 2026 dashboard design trends: glassmorphism, dark mode dominance, neon accents, card-based layouts. Full architecture plan in memory/2026/03/2026-03-04.md with code examples and file structure.

completed.title: LLM fallback stack — providers, pricing, OpenClaw integration
completed.notes: Research viable API providers for our fallback chain. Priorities: cost, tool-call reliability, OpenClaw compatibility. Candidates: Kimi K2.5 (Moonshot/OpenRouter), DeepSeek, Mistral, Groq, Together AI. Goal: have 3-4 solid fallbacks configured so we never go dark when Anthropic rate limits hit. Include setup steps for OpenClaw config.
completed.findings: Comprehensive LLM fallback research: DeepSeek V3 ($0.14/$0.28/M, unstable tools), Kimi K2.5 ($0.38/.48/M, reliable tools), Groq (free 500K/day, 1000 TPS, reliable), Mistral ($0.25/$0.75/M). Recommended 4-tier chain: Opus → Kimi K2.5 (OpenRouter) → Groq Llama 3.3 70B → DeepSeek. Cost savings 65% (light) to $100/mo (power users). OpenRouter built-in support (no providers config). Setup: env vars + fallbacks array + gateway restart. Tool calling critical: Kimi/Groq excellent, DeepSeek unreliable (looped calls). Full config + verification steps documented.

completed.findings: Both sites live on Oracle. Separate nginx configs and webroots. adamdunstan.com/html/ and shoots.adamdunstan.com/html/. SSL via Lets Encrypt.

completed.title: Print-on-demand service comparison for low-volume photographer
completed.notes: Requirements: No monthly fees, dropship/POD, Australia shipping, free hosted storefront option. Low volume expected - 'might as well' feature. Compare Printful, Prodigi, Gelato, Printify.
completed.findings: Recommendation: PRODIGI. Fine Art Trade Guild approved, local AU fulfillment partner, zero monthly fees, museum-quality prints, 80% orders ready 24-48h. Best for photography/fine art focus. Gelato as alternative for broader product range.

completed.title: Meta Graph API for Instagram feed - read-only implementation
completed.notes: Key question: Confirm read-only access scope available. No write/post permissions needed. Research: OAuth scopes, token storage, rate limits, refresh process, implementation approach.
completed.findings: CONFIRMED: Read-only access available via Instagram Graph API. Basic Display API deprecated Dec 2024. Requires Business/Creator account. Permissions: instagram_basic + instagram_graph_user_profile. 60-day token refresh required. 200 req/hour rate limit. Store tokens server-side only.

completed.title: Research proper privacy policy content for photography website
completed.findings: Required sections: data collected, purpose, model release reference, data protection, third-party sharing, user rights (access/delete), cookies, contact info. AU Privacy Act applies. GDPR if EU visitors (photos = personal data). Use Termly generator or AU template. Include model release cross-reference and gallery retention terms.

completed.title: research and suggest a privacy policy to use on shoots website

completed.title: investigate Playwright MCP - full browser automation
completed.findings: Microsoft official MCP server. Uses accessibility tree (no vision needed). CLI+SKILLS more token-efficient for coding; MCP better for exploratory/persistent automation.

completed.title: investigate Playwright Scraper - web scraping with anti-bot protection
completed.findings: Playwright handles anti-bot natively via real browser fingerprinting. Use headed mode and realistic configs for best results.
